description:
Filed 2026-05-25 from bl-203e (Phase 1A) implementation, which had to invent a path for runtime state SPEC-clone-layout §3 does not name.

## Context

SPEC §3 names four per-clone subdirs under `~/.local/state/balls/`:

- `worktrees/<nested>/<bl-id>/` — git worktrees per task
- `claims/<nested>/` — claim files
- `locks/<nested>/` — per-task flocks
- `plugins-auth/<nested>/` — plugin credentials

The pre-XDG `.balls/local/` had a few inhabitants the four above do not cover:

| inhabitant | what it is | lifetime | SPEC §3 home? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `config.json` (LocalConfig) | per-clone overrides of `require_remote_on_*` and plugin participant policy | persistent | yes — moves into `clone.json` per §6.4 |
| `tasks_dir` | one-line absolute path; stealth marker | persistent | yes — moves into `clone.json` per §4.1 |
| `pending-sync/` | human-gate queue: events awaiting forge delivery, plugin sync, etc. | persistent (mustn't lose) | **none** |
| `seen-claim-sync-policy` | one-shot marker the user has seen the claim-sync policy hint | persistent | **none** |
| `squash-<pid>/` | scratch worktree for bare-clone squash | ephemeral (PID-scoped) | **none** |

The first two land cleanly in `clone.json`. The remaining three have no SPEC home.

## What Phase 1A did

`xdg_discover.rs` populates `Store::local_dir` with `~/.local/state/balls/state/<nested>/` — a fifth per-clone subdir that does not appear in SPEC §3. The three orphaned consumers point at it. This is SPEC drift; bl-203e shipped it anyway because the alternatives are worse without a SPEC decision (`bl claim` and `bl review` need somewhere to write).

## What this ball decides

Pick one — each is a revision of SPEC-clone-layout §3 plus, for the cross-doc paths, SPEC-tracker-state §13:

(a) **Add a fifth per-clone subdir.** SPEC §3 grows by one row, e.g. `state/<nested>/` or `runtime/<nested>/`. Phase 1A's path is blessed. Simplest. SPEC's "transparent paths" invariant survives.

(b) **Split each consumer.** `pending-sync/` becomes its own SPEC dir (it is structurally identical to `claims/` — a queue of per-clone files); the marker file moves into `clone.json` (it is one bit of per-clone state); squash scratch moves to `/tmp/` (PID-suffixed, ephemeral; never wanted to survive a reboot). Three changes, but each lands in the right semantic bucket.

(c) **Move transient state to the cache root.** SPEC §3 reserves `~/.cache/balls/` "for derived/regenerable artifacts. Empty in this layout; reserved." Squash scratch and (arguably) the marker file fit. Pending-sync does not (losing it would lose work). A hybrid: pending-sync → fifth per-clone subdir; marker + scratch → cache.

Recommendation from the bl-203e author: (b). The four current per-clone dirs each express a clean concept (worktrees, claims, locks, credentials). A fifth dir named "state" or "local" is the same shape as the old `.balls/local/` catch-all that the XDG SPEC was written to avoid — naming it does not make it less of a catch-all. (b) preserves the "one dir, one purpose" property and surfaces that `pending-sync` is a queue (sibling of `claims/`), not a settings bucket.

## Output

A SPEC-clone-layout revision (sub-bl-e9c2 style) settling the four orphans' homes, plus the SPEC §14 conformance row that gates them. Blocks bl-e802 #6 ("local_dir SPEC formalization") which inherits whichever decision lands here.
title: SPEC clarification: per-clone runtime state outside the four named subdirs
